mathematical_status.purpose: force breadth, explicit invariants, and falsifiable search structure before implementation inspection

concurrent_interpretations.name: release_transaction
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: WS-08 is a release-state machine whose output is an independently verifiable exact-SHA manifest, not merely a successful deployment command.

concurrent_interpretations.name: destructive_failure_boundary
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: The decisive risk is partial destructive success across containers, networks, migrations, files, secrets, and external effects.

concurrent_interpretations.name: temporal_authority
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: Lease, expiry, retry, rate-window, and health decisions must use one authoritative clock or prove bounded skew.

concurrent_interpretations.name: durable_truth
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: Application projections may validate database-returned state but must not rewrite or improve database-authoritative outcomes.

concurrent_interpretations.name: operator_recovery
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: A technically repairable system is incomplete if an operator cannot identify the original identity, choose the safe action, and verify the result.

concurrent_interpretations.name: whole_release_rollback
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: Rollback must operate over one release unit containing code, images, configuration, migration compatibility, secrets, and proof continuity.

concurrent_interpretations.name: capacity_groundwork
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: WS-09 groundwork should expose saturation, fairness, queue, connection, and SSE pressure without claiming 25–30 employee acceptance.

concurrent_interpretations.name: pilot_governance
concurrent_interpretations.interpretation: Pilot readiness begins with stop authority, eligibility, rollback, incident ownership, data handling, and exit schemas rather than launch prose.

reusable_templates.name: partial_effect_ledger
reusable_templates.instantiate: For each destructive command, enumerate intent, first irreversible effect, durable receipt, crash points, retry permission, repair owner, and terminal evidence.

reusable_templates.name: release_identity_chain
reusable_templates.instantiate: Bind Git SHA, source tree, migration head, compose hash, config hash, image digests, manifest signature, and verifier result.

reusable_templates.name: restore_continuity
reusable_templates.instantiate: Bind backup ID, database snapshot, filesystem snapshot, secret version, restore target, migration compatibility, and post-restore proof refinding.

reusable_templates.name: authority_clock
reusable_templates.instantiate: For each lease, expiry, retry, health, and rate decision, identify the clock authority, skew assumptions, stored timestamps, and stale-decision rejection.

reusable_templates.name: topology_health
reusable_templates.instantiate: Define the full release topology and deny healthy status when any required service, network, dependency, migration, or identity binding is absent.

reusable_templates.name: rollback_compatibility
reusable_templates.instantiate: For each release mutation, prove backward, forward, or explicitly one-way compatibility and preserve accepted work across rollback.

reusable_templates.name: operator_actionability
reusable_templates.instantiate: Every ambiguous, failed, degraded, repairing, restored, rolled-back, saturated, or blocked state must expose identity, evidence, recommended action, authority, and terminal condition.

reusable_templates.name: saturation_fairness
reusable_templates.instantiate: Measure per-employee and global CPU, memory, connections, queues, SSE clients, wait time, work conservation, noisy-neighbor impact, and stop thresholds.

hypotheses.statement: More than one production compose or deployment entrypoint can claim canonical authority.
The current release unit does not bind all five required services to one exact Git SHA and immutable image digest set.
A partial topology can currently pass health or smoke checks.
At least one repair path can create a new effect identity rather than reconciling the original identity.
Rollback is service-local and can leave code, images, configuration, or migrations on different release identities.
Backup and restore do not prove refindable owner proof after database and filesystem recovery.
Secret versions are not included in restore or release proof, allowing technically successful recovery with stale or incompatible credentials.
Host Provisioner is not the sole transitive Docker authority.
Manager or Model Gateway can attach to employee networks without an explicit scoped authority record.
Production egress is broader than required and lacks an inspectable bounded policy.
Temporal decisions mix process clock and database clock, permitting stale lease, expiry, or retry behavior under skew.
Application code rewrites or normalizes database-authoritative returned records instead of validating them.
Fault injection uses unstable line or timing hooks rather than named crash points bound to semantic transitions.
Release provenance is self-asserted and lacks an independent verifier with a separate trust boundary.
Build reproducibility is not checked by rebuilding the same exact SHA and comparing declared artifacts.
The current CI does not build and inspect Manager, Gateway, Provisioner, Web, and Caddy as one release candidate.
Capacity harnesses count requests but omit open connections, SSE clients, queues, per-employee fairness, and noisy-neighbor effects.
Saturation can render as generic failure or healthy degradation without an explicit operator-visible state.
Pilot controls lack machine-readable stop authority, rollback target, incident owner, data boundary, and customer exit criteria.
The inherited WS-07 migration still contains an unqualified rate-window update that fails under PL/pgSQL output-column namespace rules.

invariants: one canonical production compose and deployment entrypoint
Host Provisioner alone owns Docker authority
employee networks are attached only through explicit scoped authority
release images are bound to exact Git SHA and immutable digests
crash after partial destructive effect never reports false success
accepted effects are never duplicated by repair or rollback
repair and reconciliation preserve original command/effect/provider identity
backup and restore include database, filesystem, secret version, and proof refinding
fault injection points are stable semantic labels
secrets never enter browser, artifact, image, manifest plaintext, or logs
capacity and pilot groundwork cannot be promoted to WS-09 acceptance

software_hyperedges.required_completion: every member is exact-candidate consistent and stale prose does not contradict executable evidence
all consequential temporal decisions use database authority or an explicit bounded-skew contract
ambiguity reaches one explicit terminal branch before retry or release
application validates returned durable state and preserves audit/conservation
repair converges on the original effect without redispatch
restored state yields the same accepted work and refindable proof

expected_closure_regressions: fixing exact image identity may expose floating tags in local development or workflows
making health topology-complete may turn previously green partial smoke tests red
whole-release rollback may reveal irreversible migration assumptions
independent manifest verification may expose non-deterministic config or generated timestamps
backup/restore proof refinding may expose storage references not captured by current backups
database-clock enforcement may change lease and retry tests that mock process time
typed failure states may break UI tests expecting generic failed or healthy states
capacity telemetry may expose unbounded SSE or connection accumulation
repair identity hardening may reject old repair jobs missing original effect lineage
secret-version binding may invalidate ad hoc operator restore procedures

operator: one exact release identity to inspect, roll back, restore, and verify
explicit ambiguous, degraded, repairing, restored, rolled_back, saturated, and blocked states
actionable evidence and terminal conditions instead of generic failure
product: trustworthy rollback and restore become customer-facing reliability capabilities
proof refinding after restore enables durable work-history guarantees
pilot stop and exit controls reduce customer lock-in and incident ambiguity
architecture: release state becomes a durable domain model rather than a collection of scripts
fault injection points become stable interfaces
capacity pressure becomes measurable per employee and per shared dependency
future_capacity: 25–30 runtimes can be evaluated with explicit connection, queue, memory, CPU, and fairness budgets
200–500 employee pressure informs sharding and control-plane separation without implementation or acceptance claims

explicit_unknowns.unknown: whether Caddy is built or only pulled
backup coverage of filesystem and object storage references
secret version persistence and rotation compatibility
current capacity harness metrics and thresholds
signing keys and independent verification trust boundary
